per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, besides the stomach pain and diarrhea, I've also been experiencing a low-grade fever a few times a week, it's not super high but it's just something that's always lingering in the back of my mind. And to be honest, I've also had some joint pain, achy knees and ankles.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I think I see what's going on here. You're trying to get all the facts out of me before we even start figuring out what's wrong with me, right? Well, let me tell you, I've been feeling really unwell for a while now - my stomach hurts, I'm having diarrhea, and I just feel tired all the time. And yeah, I have had some low-grade fevers, but like I said, they're not super high, just kind of lingering in the background.

As for night sweats, no, I don't think so... unless you count waking up from nocturnal diarrhea as a night sweat? That's been happening to me too, and it's really disrupting my sleep.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I drink 2-3 beers most weeknights, but I have to admit that lately, I've increased it to 4-5 per night due to stress from my symptoms and work. As for coping with stress, I try to exercise or go for a walk when I can.
